intents.name: event_dates
patterns: What are the dates for Hack Horizon?
response_templates: Hack Horizon is a {duration} hackathon taking place on {start_date} and {end_date}[cite: 459, 533].

intents.name: registration_fee
patterns: How much is the registration fee?
Is there a cost to register?
What is the fee per team?
Do we have to pay to participate?

intents.name: registration_deadline
patterns: When is the last date to register?
response_templates: Make sure to register before the deadline on {deadline}[cite: 610].

intents.name: team_size
patterns: What is the team size limit?
How many members can be in a team?
response_templates: You must register as a team with a minimum of {min_members} members and a maximum of {max_members} members[cite: 590].

intents.name: eligibility
patterns: Am I eligible for this hackathon?
Is this open to all college students?

data.requirement: enrolled in a recognizable educational institution

response_templates: The hackathon is open to anyone currently {requirement}[cite: 589].

intents.name: prize_pool
patterns: How much is the prize pool?
response_templates: Hack Horizon features a total prize pool of {amount}[cite: 681].

intents.name: themes
patterns: Which domains can we build projects on?

data.theme_list: Agriculture & Food, Governance & Civic Tech, Cities & Infrastructure, Community & Social Impact, Health & Wellness, and Environment & Sustainability

response_templates: The themes for Hack Horizon are: {theme_list}[cite: 631, 632, 634, 635, 636, 637].

intents.name: problem_statements
patterns: Can you give me examples of problems to solve?
What kind of projects are expected?

data.problems: 1. Environmental Risk Alert App, 2. Personal Medical History Vault, 3. Smart Campus Services Hub, 4. Document-Based Adaptive Quiz and Flashcard System

response_templates: Here are a few sample problem statements provided for the hackathon: {problems}[cite: 397, 405, 417, 424].

intents.name: venue_location
response_templates: The event will be held completely {mode} at the {venue} campus[cite: 441, 459].

intents.name: facilities
patterns: What facilities are there during the 24 hours?

data.perks: free accommodation, dinner, refreshments on both days, and high-speed internet

intents.name: rules

data.key_rules: Teams must create a new GitHub repo, push code at intervals, build an original solution, and stay on campus until the closing ceremony

response_templates: A few important rules: {key_rules}. Don't forget to bring your own laptops and a valid ID! [cite: 594, 595, 596, 597, 599]

intents.name: contact_info
patterns: How can I contact the organizers?
Who do I call for doubts?
response_templates: For any queries, you can email the organizing team at {email}[cite: 790].
